Title: Accelerated ex situ breeding of GBSS- and PTST1-edited cassava for modified starch

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Does not directly alter starch metabolism; accelerates flowering in edited cassava, enabling rapid segregation of transgene-free progeny carrying gbss edits for modified starch breeding
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in glasshouse-grown transgenic cassava leaves; flowering observed within 12 weeks under glasshouse conditions
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio SMRT sequencing of GBSS/PTST1 amplicons; NGS genome data of cassava cv. 60444 used for off-target prediction; no accession reported
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transformation; semiquantitative RT-PCR of transgene; flowering phenotype observation; PCR screening for T-DNA; segregation analysis in S1 progeny
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Constitutive AtFT expression induced early flowering in transgenic cassava, whereas WT did not flower under glasshouse conditions. 2. Early flowering enabled selfing/crossing of edited lines and recovery of transgene-free S1 progeny inheriting edited GBSS alleles. 3. This accelerated breeding workflow shortens development of modified-starch cassava lines.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enabling trait for rapid ex situ breeding of genome-edited cassava and potentially other recalcitrant crops; can be paired with trait-editing cassettes to accelerate fixation and segregation of useful recessive alleles.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtFT is an enabling breeding gene rather than a direct determinant of amylose biosynthesis, but it is a core experimental component in the study’s accelerated breeding system.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major determinant of starch physicochemical behavior during cooking and industrial processing
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Amylose biosynthesis branch of starch synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycosyltransferase; starch synthase family; granule-bound enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Amylose glucan chains within starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream polymer elongation step within starch granule
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established as the key/terminal determinant for amylose synthesis in cassava storage roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active in storage roots based on strong phenotypic effects on root starch
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glasshouse-grown plants harvested after 6 months; storage roots analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 targeted mutagenesis; PacBio amplicon sequencing; iodine staining; iodine colorimetry for amylose; immunoblotting of granule-bound GBSS; rheological starch profiling; inheritance analysis in S1 progeny
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Targeted mutagenesis of cassava GBSS generated lines ranging from WT-like to strongly reduced and fully amylose-free starch. 2. Four gbss lines produced no detectable amylose and lacked detectable granule-bound GBSS protein. 3. Partial/in-frame edits reduced but did not abolish GBSS function, producing intermediate amylose contents and altered starch pasting properties. 4. Edited GBSS alleles were heritable and recovered in transgene-free S1 progeny.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss or reduction of GBSS decreases or eliminates amylose content in cassava storage-root starch and shifts starch pasting behavior toward lower peak temperature and higher viscosity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Some variation in plant height and reduced fresh root mass were observed among transgenic lines; amylose-free lines sometimes showed increased proportion of large starch granules, while amylopectin chain-length distribution remained largely unchanged
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio SMRT sequencing of GBSS target amplicons; in-house NGS genome data of cassava cv. 60444 for target/off-target analysis; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In-frame edited alleles in gbss-TAO and a 56-bp deletion-associated allele in gbss-TAB likely retained partial function, revealing structure-function information for GBSS. No off-target indels were detected at examined sites.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for producing amylose-free or low-amylose cassava starch tailored to food and industrial uses; useful alone or in allele-series breeding for tunable starch functionality; homozygous knockout is most effective.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Influences starch physicochemical properties important for food and industrial processing
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Amylose biosynthesis module controlling GBSS deposition/localization to starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Non-enzymatic starch-binding protein required for efficient localization/association of GBSS with starch granules, thereby supporting amylose synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Accessory control step for amylose synthesis within starch granule
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control factor for efficient amylose synthesis, but not sufficient alone to abolish amylose in cassava roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional in storage roots based on mutant starch phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glasshouse-grown plants harvested after 6 months; storage roots analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 targeted mutagenesis of CBM48-coding region; PacBio amplicon sequencing; iodine colorimetry; GBSS immunoblotting from purified starch; starch rheology; starch granule and amylopectin analyses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PTST1 editing reduced amylose content in cassava storage-root starch, but no ptst line was amylose-free. 2. Reduced amylose in ptst lines correlated with decreased amount of granule-bound GBSS protein. 3. The data demonstrate that cassava PTST1 participates in amylose synthesis, likely by affecting GBSS association with starch. 4. In cassava, complete PTST1 loss has a weaker effect than in Arabidopsis leaves, indicating organ/species dependence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation of PTST1 lowers GBSS accumulation on starch granules and reduces amylose content to intermediate levels in cassava storage roots
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Effects on starch functionality were more modest than GBSS knockout; no major changes in amylopectin chain-length distribution or granule morphology were reported
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio SMRT sequencing of PTST1 target amplicons; in-house NGS genome data of cassava cv. 60444 for off-target analysis; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Target site was placed in the 3' coding region encoding the CBM48 domain, previously known to be required for starch binding in Arabidopsis. In cassava roots, both PTST1 alleles likely need inactivation for significant reduction, but even then amylose is not fully abolished, unlike Arabidopsis leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to create low-amylose intermediate starch types with commercial potential distinct from amylose-free gbss starch; may be valuable for fine-tuning starch properties or stacking with other starch genes.
